chapter_title.ar: باب الْيَمِينِ الَّتِي تَلْزَمُ صَاحِبَهَا الْكَفَّارَةُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 12 – The oath which necessitates the expiation upon its swearer

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/324)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/324)
text: كُلُّ يَمِينٍ حَلَفْتَ عَلَيْهَا لَكَ فِيهَا مَنْفَعَةٌ فِي أَمْرِ دِينٍ أَوْ دُنْيَا فَلَا شَيْ‏ءَ عَلَيْكَ فِيهَا وَ إِنَّمَا تَقَعُ عَلَيْكَ الْكَفَّارَةُ فِيمَا حَلَفْتَ عَلَيْهِ فِيمَا لِلَّهِ مَعْصِيَةٌ أَنْ لَا تَفْعَلَهُ ثُمَّ تَفْعَلُهُ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Ibn Fazzal, from Ibn Bukeyr, from Zurara,

Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> has said: ‘Every oath sword upon for you in which there is benefit for you regarding a matter of Religion or world, so there is nothing upon you with regards to it. But rather, the expiation falls upon you regarding what you have sword upon regarding what is disobedience to Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, if you do not do it, then you (end up) doing it’.
en.sarwar: 1. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Faddal from ibn Bukayr from Zurarah who has narrated the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Any oath that you take and it is for your benefits in the worldly or matters of hereafter, then there is nothing on you. Expiation is payable only when you take an oath in disobedience to Allah if you did not do it then you do it.”’
